turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: 2.	What are the new roles of IT capabilities dimensions in digital transformations of hospitals in light of challenge of achieving strategic alignment?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: What are the newly emerging roles of IT capabilities dimensions in the digital transformation of hospitals, particularly with regard to addressing challenges in achieving strategic alignment?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: 2.	What are the new roles of IT capabilities dimensions in digital transformations of hospitals in light of addressing the challenge of strategic alignment?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: What are the revised roles of IT capability dimensions in the digital transformation of hospitals, specifically to address the challenge of achieving strategic alignment?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Overall, DT plays a fundamental role in healthcare by creating new business models and opportunities that lead to value creation and address medical practice issues related to people's health (Elton & O’Riordan, 2016). Recently, it has gained prominence among researchers and practitioners (Kraus et al., 2021; Reis et al., 2018), particularly due to the Covid 19 pandemic. The Covid pandemic created an opportunity and a need to accelerate digital transformation in the healthcare industry (Steinhauser, 2021) to enable healthcare systems to address with the significant impact of the pandemic  on the daily routine of people worldwide. Today, digital transformation in healthcare is rapidly spreading and consolidating  (Stoumpos et al., 2023). Despite the growing importance of digital transformation in healthcare, a review of existing studies identified several research gaps; in particular, empirical research on the drivers of digital transformation is still lacking (Kraus et al., 2021b).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Digital transformation plays a critical role in the healthcare industry by creating novel business models and opportunities that foster value creation and address medical issues related to people's health (Elton & O'Riordan, 2016). Recently, it has garnered increased attention from researchers and practitioners alike (Kraus et al., 2021; Reis et al., 2018), particularly in light of the Covid-19 pandemic. The pandemic hastened the need and opportunity for digital transformation in healthcare (Steinhauser, 2021) to enable healthcare systems to cope with the significant impact of the pandemic on people's everyday lives worldwide. Currently, digital transformation in healthcare is rapidly gaining ground and consolidating (Stoumpos et al., 2023). However, despite the growing importance of digital transformation in healthcare, a review of current studies reveals several research gaps, with particular emphasis on a lack of empirical research on the drivers of digital transformation (Kraus et al., 2021b).
